_comment: Example config for a babylon-v2 vote: secp256k1 key ownership + M2 hash160 Merkle eligibility.
title: Should the treasury fund this initiative? (50 ETH)
description: Demo vote requiring M2 secp256k1 key ownership proof (hash160 Merkle tree).

_notes.eligibilityMode: 'babylon-v2' maps to circuit TOKEN mode (1). The M2 Merkle root is loaded from snapshotRootPath and stored in VoteConfig.token_address as an encoded Aztec Field.
_notes.snapshotRootPath: Optional. Defaults to snapshot/merkle-root-v2.json. Relative to repo root.
_notes.minTokenBalance: Optional. Overrides the minBalance in the snapshot JSON (in ubbn).
_notes.voter_calls: cast_vote_babylon_v2(vote_choice, balance, merkle_path[20], merkle_indices[20], pubkey_x[32], pubkey_y[32], sig[64])
_notes.sig_requirement: Voter must sign sha256(encode_field(title_hash) || encode_field(root_field)) with their secp256k1 key. Normalize s to low-S form (BIP-62) before passing to circuit.
